data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers clean, understated data visualization over colorful or detailed graphs
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward sparse, monochromatic interfaces with fine linework; avoid visual noise in data displays
facade.rejectImplies: User wants richer, more colored, or more detailed visual feedback; may prefer layered information density

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers clean, understated data visualization over colorful or detailed graphs
evidence.implication: Lean toward sparse, monochromatic interfaces with fine linework; avoid visual noise in data displays

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboard users prefer clean, thin-stroke data visualization over heavy bars or dense graphs
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward sparse, refined aesthetics; thin lines, whitespace, restrained color; possibly monochrome or duotone
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer denser data density, bolder visual hierarchy, or richer color coding to distinguish metrics at a glance

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated flourishes between state changes over instant updates
facade.label: Smooth data transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use easing and timing to guide attention through data changes, card animations when metrics update, smooth color transitions
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should snap between states instantly, no motion between views, direct visual feedback without interpolation

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation with information chunked into card grids rather than tabs across the top
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore variants: how wide should sidebar be? Should cards stack on mobile or stay in grid?
facade.rejectImplies: Test tab-based navigation or full-width list layouts instead

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, encouraging voice that speaks like a supportive coach rather than a clinical data dashboard
facade.label: Warm, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into personality, wit, and casual language; celebrate wins; use exclamation points; name the dashboard something playful
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward neutral, minimal copy; let data speak for itself; use formal language; remove personality markers; clinical naming

facade.hypothesis: Users want to see health data progression over time rather than just today's metrics
facade.axisTargeted: Narrative structure — sequential history vs isolated data points
facade.label: Timeline vs snapshot
facade.acceptImplies: Build multi-week/month views with trend lines, historical context, change over time as the primary story
facade.rejectImplies: Focus on current state only — today's numbers, latest reading, what matters now without comparison

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, organic color palettes over cool or high-contrast schemes for health tracking interfaces
facade.label: Muted earth tones
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into natural, desaturated colors (sage, clay, soft brown, cream) for a calming, biological feel that grounds long-term data in nature
facade.rejectImplies: The user may prefer either cooler palettes (blues, grays) for clinical clarity, or brighter accents for energy and motivation in health dashboards
